notes_md:
**xả** = to release or let something out. It’s used for physically letting out water, gas, trash, etc., and also for releasing feelings or pressure.

It’s a verb: **xả + object** — you always “release” something.  

It can also mean to vent or blow off steam emotionally. For stress, a close synonym is **giải tỏa** (more neutral/softer).

You’ll also see it in fixed phrases like **xả lũ** (release floodwater) and **xả hàng** (clear out stock at a big discount).
exampleSentences: Anh ấy phải xả nước trong bồn sau khi sửa chữa.
Chị thường đi dạo để xả căng thẳng sau giờ làm việc.
Em nhớ xả rác đúng nơi quy định khi ra ngoài nhé.

example.en: They release water into the pond.
example.es: Ellos sueltan agua en el estanque.

definitions.vi: Giải phóng hoặc để ra ngoài một cái gì đó, thường đề cập đến nước hoặc áp lực.
definitions.en: To release or let out something, often referring to water or pressure.
definitions.es: Liberar o dejar salir algo, a menudo refiriéndose al agua o a la presión.

notes:
xả = to release or let something out. It’s used for physically letting out water, gas, trash, etc., and also for releasing feelings or pressure.

It’s a verb: xả + object — you always “release” something.  

It can also mean to vent or blow off steam emotionally. For stress, a close synonym is giải tỏa (more neutral/softer).

You’ll also see it in fixed phrases like xả lũ (release floodwater) and xả hàng (clear out stock at a big discount).
exampleSentencesTranslated: He has to release the water in the tank after repairing.
She often takes a walk to release stress after work.
Remember to release trash in the right place when going out.
